title: Circular Detection in Doubly Linked List

statement.markdown: Given the head of a doubly linked list, determine if it is a circular list. You can only traverse the list from head to the tail.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a singly linked list node, which contains an integer data and a pointer to the next node.
statement.outputFormat: Output a boolean indicating whether the list is a circle or not.
statement.notes: Do not modify the original linked list

examples.explanation: The list is not a circle.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def isCycle(self, head: ListNode) -> bool:
        if not head: return False
        slow = head
        fast = head
        while slow and fast and fast.next:
            slow = slow.next
            fast = fast.next.next
            if slow == fast: return True
        return False

# Test the solution
if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Create a test case
    node1 = ListNode(1)
    node2 = ListNode(2)
    node1.next = node2
    node2.next = node2
    solution = Solution()
    print(solution.isCycle(node1))
